answer:

 {"tool": "python_exec", "arguments": {"code": "from itertools import permutations, combinations

year = 2023

dates = ['{:02d}.{:02d}.{:02d}'.format(*i) for i in product(range(1, 13), repeat=3) if len(set(i)) == 6 and i[2] == str(year)[-2:] and len(i) == 9

print(len(list(filter(lambda x: len(set(map(int, x.split('.')[::-1]))) == 6, [x[::-1] for x in dates])))
"}}

 This code should correctly calculate the number of beautiful dates in the year 2023.
